goal: Find the missing package or get it replaced/refunded.
success_criteria: Agent verifies the shipping address, applies the 48h rule (already elapsed), opens a lost-parcel investigation ticket, sets 3-5 business day expectations, does not promise the outcome or refund instantly.

customer_msg: So the tracking says my order O1008 was delivered on June 29 but I never got anything? I checked with my neighbors already. tom.alvarez@example.com.
agent_msg: Hi Tom. I’m sorry to hear you didn’t receive your order. I see that it was shipped to **809 Alvarez St, Springfield, IL** — is that correct?

customer_msg: Yeah, that’s my address. I even checked with the mail room and they didn’t have it either.
agent_msg: Tom, I’ve pulled up your order. It was marked as delivered on June 29th, but I understand you haven’t received it. Since it’s been less than 48 hours since the delivery scan, there’s still a chance it might turn up—sometimes packages are scanned early or left in unexpected places. Could you double-check around your mailroom or with your neighbors one more time?

customer_msg: I already did that. I even asked the mailroom guy and he said he didn’t see it. My neighbors didn’t have it either. What now?
agent_msg: Tom, I’ve opened an investigation with our shipping carrier. This usually takes 3–5 business days, and I’ll keep you updated. If the package is lost, we’ll replace it right away or issue a full refund—whichever you prefer.

agent_msg: Will do, Tom! I’ll follow up as soon as I hear back from the shipping carrier. Have a great day!
